methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $300M - $3.0B.

data.narrative:
Xtend is a Series B defense and security company founded by Aviv Shapira that has raised $100M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private entities operating in the same sector and stage.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $100M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $190M. Peer-median analysis references 7 same-sector, same-stage comparables including Dream Security ($3.0B), Harmattan AI ($1.4B), Code Metal ($1.2B), Castelion ($1.0B), and Defense Unicorns ($1.0B). This peer set spans $300M to $3.0B with a median valuation of $1.0B.

The triangulated range is $162M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $595M. The substantial spread reflects meaningful dispersion within the peer cohort and divergence between stage-progression and peer-median methodologies. Key uncertainties include Xtend's relative competitive position within a broad peer set that ranges nearly 10x, the quality and timing of revenue traction relative to comparables, and whether the company's specific product-market fit aligns with high-valuation or lower-valuation peers. Additional diligence on go-to-market maturity and customer concentration would refine the range.
